evals.prompt: Create a Logseq journal entry for March 15, 2026 with 3 tasks: a NOW high-priority task to fix the login bug in Project Alpha (deadline today), a LATER medium-priority task to write API tests (scheduled for March 16), and a LATER low-priority task to update documentation. Include a morning standup notes section.
evals.expected_output: A journal file at journals/2026_03_15.md with correct outliner syntax (all content lines starting with '- '), NOW/LATER task markers with priorities [#A]/[#B]/[#C], DEADLINE and SCHEDULED dates in angle-bracket format, and no YAML frontmatter.
expectations: correct-filename: Journal file created at journals/2026_03_15.md with yyyy_MM_dd format
now-priority-a: Has a NOW [#A] task for the login bug
later-priority-b: Has a LATER [#B] task for API tests
later-priority-c: Has a LATER [#C] task for documentation
deadline-syntax: Uses DEADLINE: <2026-03-15 ...> with angle bracket date format
scheduled-syntax: Uses SCHEDULED: <2026-03-16 ...> with angle bracket date format
outliner-syntax: Every content line starts with '- ' bullet prefix (no bare text lines)
no-yaml-frontmatter: Does not include YAML frontmatter (--- delimiters) at top of journal file
page-links: Uses [[Project Alpha]] page link syntax
standup-section: Includes a standup or morning notes section
two-space-indent: Uses 2-space indentation for nesting, not tabs

evals.prompt: Create a Logseq project page for 'Projects/WebApp' with sub-pages 'Projects/WebApp/Architecture' and 'Projects/WebApp/Tasks'. The main project page should have tags (project, active), a status property set to 'in-progress', and link to its sub-pages. The Architecture page should describe a React + Node.js stack. The Tasks page should have 3 tasks with different priorities.
evals.expected_output: Three markdown files using triple-underscore namespace escaping: Projects___WebApp.md, Projects___WebApp___Architecture.md, Projects___WebApp___Tasks.md. Main page has page-level properties (tags::, status::) without '- ' prefix. All content blocks use '- ' prefix. Sub-pages are linked with [[Projects/WebApp/...]] syntax.
expectations: main-page-file: Creates pages/Projects___WebApp.md with triple-underscore namespace
arch-page-file: Creates pages/Projects___WebApp___Architecture.md
tasks-page-file: Creates pages/Projects___WebApp___Tasks.md
page-tags-property: Main page has tags:: property with project and active
page-status-property: Main page has status:: in-progress property
double-colon-props: Properties use double-colon syntax (key:: value), not single colon
page-props-no-bullet: Page-level properties at file top are NOT prefixed with '- '
subpage-links: Main page links to sub-pages using [[Projects/WebApp/Architecture]] syntax
outliner-syntax: All content blocks (non-property lines) start with '- ' bullet prefix
tasks-with-priorities: Tasks page has tasks with different priority levels [#A], [#B], [#C]
two-space-indent: Uses 2-space indentation for nesting, not tabs

evals.prompt: Add an advanced Datalog query to a Logseq page called 'Dashboards/Active Tasks' that finds all NOW and LATER tasks from the past 7 days, sorted by priority. Also add a simple query below it that finds all tasks tagged with #urgent.
evals.expected_output: A page at pages/Dashboards___Active Tasks.md containing a #+BEGIN_QUERY/#+END_QUERY advanced Datalog query with :block/marker filtering for NOW/LATER, date range inputs, sort-by priority result-transform, and a separate {{query ...}} simple inline query for #urgent tasks.
expectations: correct-filename: Creates pages/Dashboards___Active Tasks.md with triple-underscore namespace
datalog-begin-end: Uses #+BEGIN_QUERY and #+END_QUERY delimiters for advanced query
block-marker-filter: Datalog query filters on :block/marker for NOW and LATER
date-inputs-bounded: Query uses :inputs with both a start bound (e.g. :7d-before, :14d) AND an end bound (e.g. :today) to properly scope the date range
sort-by-priority: Includes result-transform with sort-by on :block/priority
simple-query: Has a {{query ...}} simple inline query
urgent-reference: Simple query references #urgent or [[urgent]]
outliner-syntax: All content lines start with '- ' bullet prefix
two-space-indent: Uses 2-space indentation for nesting, not tabs
